Session Title:: Optional Early Bird Workshop: Installing the REDCap Cypress Developer Toolkit
Session Description w/ (Audience)::
For a list of pre-requisites / requirements needed before attending this workshop, please go to: https://redcap.link/cypressworkshop . 
Prepare your developer laptop for a future filled with REDCap automated testing by installing the REDCap Cypress Developer Toolkit. This session provides step-by-step guidance and in-person assistance for installing the toolkit, which contains all components of the test environment necessary to run REDCap Cypress Test Framework (RCTF) on a developer machine, providing a common setup to locally develop and execute automated feature tests. RCTF is capable of executing feature tests for external modules and site-specific functionality. This workshop is ideal for those looking to help with Regulatory and Software Validation Committee efforts to develop feature tests and those who wish to develop their own external module feature tests or site-specific feature tests written in the Gherkin domain-specific language and powered by RCTF.  Note: This workshop only covers the installation of the developer toolkit.  It does NOT cover how to write automated feature tests. Please attend Optional Early Bird Workshop: "Mastering Gherkin for REDCap Automated Testing" to learn how to write Gherkin features. (Technical)
_raw: Optional Early Bird Workshop: Installing the REDCap Cypress Developer Toolkit

For a list of pre-requisites / requirements needed before attending this workshop, please go to: https://redcap.link/cypressworkshop .

RCTF is capable of executing feature tests for external modules and site-specific functionality. This workshop is ideal for those looking to help with Regulatory and Software Validation Committee efforts to develop feature tests and those who wish to develop their own external module feature tests or site-specific feature tests written in the Gherkin domain-specific language and powered by RCTF.  Note: This workshop only covers the installation of the developer toolkit.  It does NOT cover how to write automated feature tests. Please attend Optional Early Bird Workshop: "Mastering Gherkin for REDCap Automated Testing" to learn how to write Gherkin features. (Technical)

Session Title:: Leveraging REDCap for Academically-Led Multicenter Cohort Studies and Clinical Trials in Pediatric Rare Disease: The CHAT Consortium and Kids-DOTT Investigator Group Experiences
Presenter Information:: Dr. Neil Goldenberg and Dr. Julie Jaffray
Session Description w/ (Audience):: We will discuss how the use of REDCap by two national/multinational networks committed to advancing the field of thromboembolism prevention and treatment in patients <21 years is empowering clinical coordinating center and data coordinating center partnerships in NIH-, Foundation- and industry-sponsored multicenter cohort studies and clinical trials. (All Attendees)

Session Title:: MyCap Setup Deep Dive: Learn to use basic and advanced features
Session Description w/ (Audience)::
During this session, we aim to equip REDCap Admins with the knowledge needed to understand how MyCap works and support their local study teams. 

We will cover the following topics:
* What is MyCap?
* What are studies 'of good fit' and typical use cases?
* Review of basic features and REDCap feature compatibility 
* Demonstrate how to setup, test, and modify MyCap on a longitudinally-enabled REDCap project
* Tips, tricks, and resources for supporting MyCap as a REDCap Administrator  (Features/Functions)

During this session, we aim to equip REDCap Admins with the knowledge needed to understand how MyCap works and support their local study teams. 

We will cover the following topics:
* What is MyCap?
* What are studies 'of good fit' and typical use cases?
* Review of basic features and REDCap feature compatibility 
* Demonstrate how to setup, test, and modify MyCap on a longitudinally-enabled REDCap project
* Tips, tricks, and resources for supporting MyCap as a REDCap Administrator  (Features/Functions)

(New Administrator Focused)

Session Title:: Understanding REDCap Logic & Syntax (where and how it's used)
Session Description w/ (Audience):: This workshop is designed to equip new to moderately experienced REDCap administrators with a solid understanding of REDCap syntax. We will delve into the fundamentals of syntax, exploring its applications in calculations, branching logic, and Automated Survey Invitations (ASI). Participants will learn common techniques, best practices, and creative ways to leverage REDCap's full potential. For those seeking advanced knowledge, we will also briefly explore more complex syntax options. (New Administrator Focused)

For those seeking advanced knowledge, we will also briefly explore more complex syntax options. (New Administrator Focused)

Session Title:: REDCap Validation: How to Use the RSVC Materials (RSVC: Regulatory & Software Validation Committee)
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Explore the practical applications of RSVC materials in enhancing your REDCap projects' compliance and efficiency. This session will guide you through the comprehensive suite of RSVC resources, demonstrating how to effectively implement these tools in your workflows to meet regulatory standards and streamline project management. Attendees will learn step-by-step methods to utilize validation packages, SOPs, and compliance tools provided by the RSVC, ensuring their projects not only meet but exceed the rigor of industry and regulatory expectations. Join us to transform your approach to REDCap project management with expert insights and proven strategies. (Other / Both Tech and Admin)

Session Title:: Harnessing REDCap: Transforming Data into Dynamic Dashboards for Operational Excellence and Strategic Insight
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Re-discover the power of REDCap in our session on "Harnessing REDCap: Transforming Data into Dynamic Dashboards for Operational Excellence and Strategic Insight". We'll explore how REDCap enables the creation of innovative data visualizations for decision-making, research, and efficiency. Attendees will gain practical insights into leveraging REDCap for strategic data management and dashboards, revolutionizing their organization's data landscape. (All Attendees)

Session Description w/ (Audience):: AFMC developed a naloxone reporting tool, enabling first responders to report naloxone administrations while collecting demographic, location, and administration data, and sending alerts when a certain number of administrations occur within a specified time period. We hope to educate attendees on the best practices for setting up and disseminating a public survey for statewide data collection, validating the data, and disseminating the data to key stakeholders. (All Attendees)

(All Attendees)

Session Title:: 2 Years to 70 Projects on FHIR - Advice on Getting Institutional buy-in and facilitating FHIR usage in research
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Review 2+ years of promoting and supporting FHIR projects at a large research foundation, including lessons learned when growing the REDCap / FHIR utilization to 70 research studies. We will discuss strategies around how to get buy-in from your institution, highlight built-in data protections in the FHIR / Epic interface, training cadence and organization, the difference between Data Mart and Clinical Data tools in practical research application, application of FHIR custom mapping tool, highlight when FHIR works well and provide support and encouragement to any admin looking to make the REDCap / FHIR integration work for your institution.  (All Attendees)

Session Title:: The State of REDCap Automated Testing
Session Description w/ (Audience):: From ambitious dream to software reality: embark on a brief but informative review of the six-year journey to create the REDCap Cypress Test Framework (RCTF) as it stands today. Discover how RCTF evolved from a simple institutional REDCap testing platform to a sophisticated Gherkin-powered tool developed for and embraced by the Regulatory and Software Validation Committee. Learn about the framework's current state, the significant impact it has made, the challenges faced, lessons learned, the future vision for RCTF and how you can help advance automated testing. (All Attendees)
_raw: The State of REDCap Automated Testing

From ambitious dream to software reality: embark on a brief but informative review of the six-year journey to create the REDCap Cypress Test Framework (RCTF) as it stands today. Discover how RCTF evolved from a simple institutional REDCap testing platform to a sophisticated Gherkin-powered tool developed for and embraced by the Regulatory and Software Validation Committee. Learn about the framework's current state, the significant impact it has made, the challenges faced, lessons learned, the future vision for RCTF and how you can help advance automated testing. (All Attendees)

Session Title:: REDCap to the Rescue! Improving data management for advanced visual analytics
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Discover how to build a REDCap "Master List of Measures" for your institution to streamline the collection, storage, and retrieval of metadata for visual analytics data models. Learn how REDCap empowered both end users and analysts at Johns Hopkins All Children's Hospital to efficiently manage metrics across research, clinical, and operational domains. Leave equipped with practical skills to integrate REDCap data with your KPIs to create advanced visual analytics tools. (Technical)

Leave equipped with practical skills to integrate REDCap data with your KPIs to create advanced visual analytics tools. (Technical)

Session Title:: The Many Hats of a REDCap Administrator
Session Description w/ (Audience):: 'REDCap Admin' has to wear many hats when you run a successful installation.  Some times you are a trainer, project builder, support person, technical guru.  Join this fun team in learning how to wear the proper hat to support your REDCap instance like a champion.   (New Administrator Focused)
_raw: The Many Hats of a REDCap Administrator

'REDCap Admin' has to wear many hats when you run a successful installation.  Some times you are a trainer, project builder, support person, technical guru.  Join this fun team in learning how to wear the proper hat to support your REDCap instance like a champion.   (New Administrator Focused)

Session Title:: eConsent 2.0 Framework + PDF Snapshot:  A Project's Best Friend
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Vanderbilt formally released its REDCap-based, 21 CFR Part 11 compliant, electronic Consent (eConsent) Framework in early 2018.  During the past six years the platform has been used to render over 5 million eConsent transactions across >3,700 institutions. The platform has been used to support rapid startup of multi-center clinical trials and the data collected during this period has led to the development of new REDCap functionalities and plugins, best practices, and spotlighted areas for opportunity.  This session will give a brief history of eConsent and its successes, describe first generation limitations within the eConsent platform, and describe the new features in the recently launched eConsent 2.0 Framework + PDF Snapshot, complete with illustrative use cases.  Attendees will benefit by understanding the basics of the REDCap-based eConsent platform and the process for developing new eConsent features.  (Features/Functions)
_raw: eConsent 2.0 Framework + PDF Snapshot:  A Project's Best Friend

Vanderbilt formally released its REDCap-based, 21 CFR Part 11 compliant, electronic Consent (eConsent) Framework in early 2018.  During the past six years the platform has been used to render over 5 million eConsent transactions across >3,700 institutions. The platform has been used to support rapid startup of multi-center clinical trials and the data collected during this period has led to the development of new REDCap functionalities and plugins, best practices, and spotlighted areas for opportunity.  This session will give a brief history of eConsent and its successes, describe first generation limitations within the eConsent platform, and describe the new features in the recently launched eConsent 2.0 Framework + PDF Snapshot, complete with illustrative use cases.  Attendees will benefit by understanding the basics of the REDCap-based eConsent platform and the process for developing new eConsent features.  (Features/Functions)

Session Title:: Developing Automated Workflows and Expanding Operational Uses of REDCap
Presenter Information:: Laurie Bailey, Alice Hoeft, Rachel Wenthe
Session Description w/ (Audience):: In this session, we will discuss how and why we have leveraged REDCap to provide operational support for a variety of projects to meet departmental needs. From an abstract submission and scoring platform to feasibility and scientific review, department directory, DUA management, and many others, we will discuss how we have used REDCap to fill the gap and drive research and quality forward.  (Administrative)

Session Title:: Extending the REDCap API with EMs and a Proposal for a REDCap API 2.0
Session Description w/ (Audience):: This session aims to continue where we left at last year's REDCapCon in "API Security & Management" session. An upcoming feature extension of the EM module framework will be presented (feature-complete, release imminent) as well as a proposed new API model (based on the JSON-RPC standard) that will include modern API security and management features (currently in draft stage, but with some working code available). The session is targeted at a more technical audience (EM developers, API creators and consumers), as well as admins concerned with aspects related to security and token management when allowing API access to their REDCap instance. (Technical)
_raw: Extending the REDCap API with EMs and a Proposal for a REDCap API 2.0

This session aims to continue where we left at last year's REDCapCon in "API Security & Management" session. An upcoming feature extension of the EM module framework will be presented (feature-complete, release imminent) as well as a proposed new API model (based on the JSON-RPC standard) that will include modern API security and management features (currently in draft stage, but with some working code available). The session is targeted at a more technical audience (EM developers, API creators and consumers), as well as admins concerned with aspects related to security and token management when allowing API access to their REDCap instance. (Technical)

Session Title:: Intro to Part 11 / Regulatory Compliance for Beginners
Session Description w/ (Audience):: Join us for an enlightening journey into the world of regulatory compliance within REDCap, designed specifically for newcomers or those seeking a refresher. This session will introduce the critical regulations that impact REDCap, including 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH GCP E6, and explain how to navigate and implement these standards effectively. Learn about ALCOA+ principles, the essential components of compliance, and how the RSVC Validation Package can be a pivotal resource in your compliance toolkit. This engaging presentation aims to equip you with the foundational knowledge to ensure your REDCap projects are compliant and audit-ready. (New Administrator Focused)

Learn about ALCOA+ principles, the essential components of compliance, and how the RSVC Validation Package can be a pivotal resource in your compliance toolkit. This engaging presentation aims to equip you with the foundational knowledge to ensure your REDCap projects are compliant and audit-ready. (New Administrator Focused)

Session Title:: Inheriting a REDCap Program: what to do next?
Session Description w/ (Audience):: In this session, we will discuss how to get started when you are coming into a new management role for an established REDCap program. We will be covering 5 different aspects of running a successful REDCap program: Infrastructure, Support, Training, Policy & Communications. For each aspect, we'll cover some common strategies, potential roadblocks, political considerations, and essential things to think about. (Administrative)

Session Description w/ (Audience):: (All Attendees)

We will access REDCap data from within R through the API, and use Quarto to generate a presentation with some beautiful charts, and Shiny to generate an interactive dashboard, all based on live data from a REDCap project. 
Special care is taken to provide simple, self-contained examples that can be easily tweaked for the attendee's own projects.  (Other / Both Tech and Admin)
_raw:
We will access REDCap data from within R through the API, and use Quarto to generate a presentation with some beautiful charts, and Shiny to generate an interactive dashboard, all based on live data from a REDCap project. 
Special care is taken to provide simple, self-contained examples that can be easily tweaked for the attendee's own projects.  (Other / Both Tech and Admin)

Session Title:: Introduction to Data Access Groups (DAGs) and their usage
Session Description w/ (Audience):: This session will introduce the Data Access Groups (DAGs) feature in REDCap projects which enables you to create multiple sites or groups and limit users to only accessing records assigned to their site. We will look at setting up DAGs, assigning users to them, enabling the ability to switch DAGs and some ways to utilize DAGs with other REDCap features to customize them for each site.  (New Administrator Focused)

Session Title:: Multi-Language Management: Brief overview and update; deep dive and future development
Session Description w/ (Audience):: This session will give a brief overview of the features of Multi-Language Management in REDCap (including the upcoming support for MyCap) and tackle a few of the more advanced usage scenarios and possible solutions. About half the time of the session will be reserved for discussion of problems, issues, possible solutions, feature gaps, and potential future enhancements. This session will not be a beginners' guide to using MLM, but is targeted at (experienced) admins who have used have gathered at least some experience with MLM in (production) projects. (Features/Functions)
_raw: Multi-Language Management: Brief overview and update; deep dive and future development

This session will give a brief overview of the features of Multi-Language Management in REDCap (including the upcoming support for MyCap) and tackle a few of the more advanced usage scenarios and possible solutions. About half the time of the session will be reserved for discussion of problems, issues, possible solutions, feature gaps, and potential future enhancements. This session will not be a beginners' guide to using MLM, but is targeted at (experienced) admins who have used have gathered at least some experience with MLM in (production) projects. (Features/Functions)

Session Description w/ (Audience):: REDCap Administrators grapple with a myriad of challenges arising from institutional and local research community dynamics. Their responsibilities span from optimizing user experience and ensuring regulatory compliance to meeting institutional demands and managing the technical aspects of REDCap. The frequent realignment of priorities and roles, task-switching, among REDCap Administrators exacerbates these challenges. Furthermore, inadequate institutional support for these responsibilities can lead to heightened stress levels and diminished work satisfaction. Recognizing the critical importance of prioritizing emotional well-being is important for fostering a positive work environment for REDCap Administrators. This session aims to serve as both a platform for sharing strategies in navigating the unique stressors of our roles as REDCap Administrators and a forum for interest gathering, potentially leading to collaborative projects within the Consortium. (Other / Both Tech and Admin)
_raw: REDCap Administrators grapple with a myriad of challenges arising from institutional and local research community dynamics. Their responsibilities span from optimizing user experience and ensuring regulatory compliance to meeting institutional demands and managing the technical aspects of REDCap. The frequent realignment of priorities and roles, task-switching, among REDCap Administrators exacerbates these challenges. Furthermore, inadequate institutional support for these responsibilities can lead to heightened stress levels and diminished work satisfaction. Recognizing the critical importance of prioritizing emotional well-being is important for fostering a positive work environment for REDCap Administrators. This session aims to serve as both a platform for sharing strategies in navigating the unique stressors of our roles as REDCap Administrators and a forum for interest gathering, potentially leading to collaborative projects within the Consortium. (Other / Both Tech and Admin)
